prose:
Computed property: Fukui f+ index of the most electrophilic site in nitrobenzene, obtained via the chemkit `fukui-reactivity` skill.

Method provenance:
• Property: condensed Fukui functions (f + , f − , f 0 ) and dual descriptor; headline value is the largest electrophilic Fukui function (f + ) = 0.49641782 on C4.
• Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` = B3LYP/def2-TZVP with default RI approximation (density fitting) in PySCF 2.x backend.
• Geometry provenance: single-point calculations (neutral, N+1, and N−1 states) performed directly on the user-supplied Cartesian geometry file `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz`; no additional geometry optimization was carried out (DFT//input-xyz protocol).
• Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model).
• Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (0, 1) for the N electron state; cation (+1, 2) and anion (−1, 2) for the N±1 states, as required for Fukui analysis.
• Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill calling PySCF (DFT B3LYP/def2-TZVP) for the electronic structure; Mulliken population analysis used for partial charges.
• Convergence: all three SCF calculations reported `converged: true` in the JSON output.
• Warnings (verbatim):
  – "sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"
  – "sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"
  – "common header present"
  (Both charge-conservation checks passed — sums are exactly 1.0000 — so the warnings are informational.)
• Live log: the run was streamed to the caller’s working directory; see the engine log emitted at launch (path provided during execution) and the result JSON saved at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpe_k4vru6/live.json` for full details.

Result highlights (atom indices 0-based, as in the output JSON):
• Most electrophilic site: atom 3 (label 4, C) with f +  = 0.49641782.
• Most nucleophilic site: atom 4 (label 5, C) with f −  = 0.10728820.

The headline value reported below is the maximum electrophilic Fukui function (f + ) for nitrobenzene at this level of theory.
